methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Endurosat is a Seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Raycho Raychev that has raised $167M+. The valuation universe includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though the immediate peer set at identical stage and valuation tier remains limited.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and yields an implied valuation of $40M. This approach uses $16M as a proxy for total capital raised to date, applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from Seed to the next financing stage. The Peer-Median method is inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables—while 23 same-sector peers exist broadly, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies yields fewer than three datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring cannot be applied because target revenue is not on file; the public Space & Aerospace median P/S stands at 64.2x across the five tickers, but this cannot be meaningfully applied without Endurosat's revenue.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $34M to $46M, with a median of $40M. This single-method result carries material uncertainty. Key sensitivities include the applicability of the 2.5x stage multiplier to this specific company's capital efficiency and market position, the absence of direct peer benchmarking, and incomplete financial disclosure typical of private companies at this stage. Additional financial detail or a expanded peer dataset would strengthen conviction in this range.
